name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.WorkoutController
comment:
REST controller for the workout module endpoints. 
 GET    /api/v1/workouts               — paginated user library GET    /api/v1/workouts/templates     — public / system templates GET    /api/v1/workouts/{id}          — full detail (steps included) POST   /api/v1/workouts               — create a new workout PUT    /api/v1/workouts/{id}          — full replacement update DELETE /api/v1/workouts/{id}          — soft delete GET    /api/v1/workouts/{id}/export/fit — export as .FIT file (💎 Pro)

All endpoints except {@code GET /workouts/templates} require authentication. Ownership is enforced in the use-case layer — users can only access/mutate their own workouts (or public/system templates on reads).

The {@code steps} field is a raw JSON array passed through verbatim to JSONB storage, preserving compatibility with a future FIT export pipeline.

No calendar logic is implemented here; see the calendar module.

methods.name: listTemplates
methods.comment:
Returns paginated public/system workout templates. Accessible without authentication (open endpoint).

Query parameters:

  • {@code sport} — optional sport filter (e.g. "cycling", "running")
  • {@code page} — 0-indexed page (default 0)
  • {@code size} — page size (default 20, max 100)

Important: this path must be declared before the {@code /{id}} mapping so Spring MVC does not try to parse "templates" as a UUID.

methods.name: list
methods.comment:
Returns the authenticated user's workout library (paginated).

Query parameters:

  • {@code sport} — optional sport filter
  • {@code isTemplate} — optional boolean filter (true = templates only)
  • {@code page} — 0-indexed page (default 0)
  • {@code size} — page size (default 20, max 100)

methods.name: get
methods.comment: Returns the full detail of a single workout, including its {@code steps} JSON. Accessible if the workout belongs to the user or is a public/system template.

methods.name: create
methods.comment:
Creates a new workout in the user's library.

Validation:

  • {@code name} and {@code sport} are required.
  • {@code steps} must be a valid JSON array conforming to the workout data model.
Returns 201 with a {@code Location} header pointing to the created resource.

methods.name: update
methods.comment: Fully replaces an existing workout. Only the owning user may update their workout. Returns 200 with the updated detail.

methods.name: delete
methods.comment: Soft-deletes a workout (sets {@code deleted_at = now()}). Returns 204 No Content on success.

methods.name: exportFit
methods.comment:
Exports the workout as a Garmin .FIT file and returns a pre-signed 24-hour download URL.

The FIT file is generated on each call and stored in MinIO {@code workout-exports} bucket. Repeated exports overwrite the previous object (same key pattern).

Response 200: 

{@code { "downloadUrl": "https://...", "filename": "Tempo_Intervals.fit" } }

The caller should initiate a file download using {@code downloadUrl}.

methods.name: validateRequestFields
methods.comment: Basic field-level validation before the use-case validator runs structural checks on the steps JSON.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.WorkoutExceptionHandler
comment:
Exception handler scoped to the workout module's inbound adapters.

Maps domain exceptions thrown during workout creation / update to the standard CoachFit error envelope ({@code { "error": { "code": "...", "message": "..." } }}).

methods.name: handleValidation
methods.comment:
Maps {@link WorkoutValidationException} → 400 Bad Request.

Thrown by {@link com.coachfit.workout.domain.WorkoutStepsValidator} when the {@code steps} JSON fails structural validation against the rules in docs/07-workout-data-model.md.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.dto.FitExportResponse
comment: Response body for {@code GET /api/v1/workouts/{id}/export/fit}.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.dto.RawJsonDeserializer
comment:
Jackson deserializer that captures a JSON value (object, array, scalar) as its raw JSON string representation.

Used for the {@code steps} field in {@link WorkoutRequest} so the workout steps array is preserved verbatim for JSONB storage without an intermediate Java object model.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.dto.WorkoutDetailResponse
comment:
Response DTO for GET /api/v1/workouts/{id}.

{@code steps} is serialised with {@code @JsonRawValue} so the JSONB string is embedded directly in the response without double-encoding.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.dto.WorkoutListResponse
comment:
Response DTO for GET /api/v1/workouts.

Steps are NOT included in the list view for performance — clients should call GET /workouts/{id} to retrieve the full workout structure.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.dto.WorkoutRequest
comment:
Request body for POST /api/v1/workouts and PUT /api/v1/workouts/{id}.

The {@code steps} field is deserialized as a raw JSON string so the JSONB value can be passed directly to the validator and persisted without intermediate object model conversion (FIT export compatibility is preserved).

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.dto.WorkoutTemplateListResponse
comment:
Response DTO for GET /api/v1/workouts/templates.

Steps are omitted from the list view; clients call GET /workouts/{id} for the full workout structure before use.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.out.persistence.WorkoutEntity
comment:
JPA entity mapping the {@code workouts} table.

The {@code steps} column is stored as JSONB and surfaced as a raw JSON string — the domain validator handles structural checking before writes, and the raw string is returned as-is to API clients.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.out.persistence.WorkoutJpaRepository
comment: Spring Data JPA repository for {@link WorkoutEntity}.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.out.persistence.WorkoutPersistenceAdapter
comment:
JPA + JdbcClient adapter implementing {@link WorkoutPersistencePort}.

JPA is used for inserts (via {@link WorkoutEntity}). JdbcClient handles reads, updates, and soft-deletes to avoid loading unnecessary fields on hot read paths.

name: com.coachfit.workout.adapter.out.storage.MinioWorkoutExportStorageAdapter
comment:
Stores generated .FIT workout exports in MinIO, implementing {@link WorkoutExportStoragePort}.

Object key pattern

{@code workouts/{userId}/{workoutId}.fit}

Re-exports of the same workout overwrite the previous file in MinIO (same key). This is intentional — the export is a derived artifact and the newest version is always the authoritative one.

The {@link MinioClient} bean is provided by the activity module's {@code MinioConfig}.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.CreateWorkoutUseCase
comment:
Input port: create a new workout (POST /api/v1/workouts).

The {@code steps} field is accepted as a raw JSON string and stored as JSONB. Structural validation is performed by {@code WorkoutStepsValidator} before the use case is invoked.

methods.name: create
methods.comment: Creates a new workout in the user's library.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.DeleteWorkoutUseCase
comment:
Input port: soft-delete a workout (DELETE /api/v1/workouts/{id}).

Sets {@code deleted_at = now()} on the workout row. Only the owning user may delete their workout. System templates (user_id IS NULL) cannot be deleted through this endpoint.

methods.name: delete
methods.comment: Soft-deletes the workout.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.ExportFitUseCase
comment:
Input port: export a workout as a Garmin .FIT file.

Implements {@code GET /api/v1/workouts/{id}/export/fit} (💎 Pro tier).

The implementation:

  1. Loads the workout (enforcing ownership).
  2. Loads the requesting user's sport zones (for relative-target resolution).
  3. Encodes the workout into binary .FIT format.
  4. Stores the .FIT file in MinIO {@code workout-exports} bucket.
  5. Returns a pre-signed 24-hour download URL.
@throws org.springframework.web.server.ResponseStatusException 404 if the workout is not found

methods.name: exportFit
methods.comment: Generates a FIT export for the given workout and returns a time-limited download URL.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.ExportFitUseCase$FitExportResult
comment: Result of a successful FIT export.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.GetWorkoutUseCase
comment:
Input port: get the full detail of a single workout (GET /api/v1/workouts/{id}).

Ownership is enforced: the user must own the workout, or the workout must be a public system template (user_id IS NULL).

methods.name: get
methods.comment: Loads the full detail of a workout.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.ListWorkoutTemplatesUseCase
comment:
Input port: list public system workout templates (GET /api/v1/workouts/templates).

Returns workouts where {@code is_template = true} AND ({@code user_id IS NULL} (system) OR {@code is_public = true}). Supports optional sport filtering and pagination.

methods.name: listTemplates
methods.comment: Returns a page of public/system workout templates.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.ListWorkoutsUseCase
comment:
Input port: list workouts in the authenticated user's library (GET /api/v1/workouts).

Returns user-owned workouts plus any public system templates the user has saved, ordered by newest first. Soft-deleted workouts are excluded.

methods.name: list
methods.comment: Returns a page of the user's workouts.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.in.UpdateWorkoutUseCase
comment:
Input port: update an existing workout (PUT /api/v1/workouts/{id}).

Only the owning user may update a workout. System templates (user_id IS NULL) may not be mutated through this use case.

methods.name: update
methods.comment: Applies a full replacement update to the workout.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.out.WorkoutExportStoragePort
comment:
Output port: storage operations for generated workout .FIT exports.

Files are stored in the MinIO {@code workout-exports} bucket. Pre-signed URLs are returned to the caller with a 24-hour expiry.

methods.name: store
methods.comment:
Stores a generated .FIT file and returns the object key.

Object key pattern: {@code workouts/{userId}/{workoutId}.fit}

methods.name: generateDownloadUrl
methods.comment: Generates a pre-signed GET URL for an already-stored export.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.port.out.WorkoutPersistencePort
comment:
Output port: workout record persistence (the {@code workouts} table).

All write methods are transactional; the adapter implements {@code @Transactional} on mutating methods.

methods.name: save
methods.signature: save(java.util.UUID, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.List, boolean, boolean, java.lang.String, java.lang.Integer, java.math.BigDecimal)
methods.comment: Persists a new workout row and returns the generated UUID.

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update(java.util.UUID, java.util.UUID, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.List, boolean, boolean, java.lang.Integer, java.math.BigDecimal)
methods.comment: Fully replaces editable fields on an existing workout.

methods.name: softDelete
methods.comment: Soft-deletes a workout owned by the user (sets {@code deleted_at = now()}).

methods.name: findDetailById
methods.comment:
Full detail view used by GET /workouts/{id}.

Returns empty if the workout is deleted or inaccessible to the user. A workout is accessible if:

  • it belongs to the user ({@code user_id = userId}), OR
  • it is a system template ({@code user_id IS NULL}) and not deleted

methods.name: list
methods.comment: Paginated list of a user's own workouts (non-deleted).

methods.name: count
methods.comment: Total count matching the list filters.

methods.name: listTemplates
methods.comment: Paginated list of public/system templates (non-deleted). Returns workouts where is_template=true AND (user_id IS NULL OR is_public=true).

methods.name: countTemplates
methods.comment: Total count of public/system templates.

name: com.coachfit.workout.application.service.WorkoutService
comment:
Application service implementing all workout use cases.

Ownership is always enforced: every mutating query includes {@code user_id = :userId} so a user can never modify another user's workouts.

Separation of concerns:

  • Read queries (list, get, templates) — no transaction needed.
  • Writes (create, update, delete) — transactional.

methods.name: buildZoneContext
methods.comment: Extracts FTP and LTHR from the athlete's zone list and wraps them in a {@link ZoneContext}. Falls back to {@link ZoneContext#defaults()} values if zones are absent or unconfigured.

name: com.coachfit.workout.domain.FitEncoder
comment:
Domain service that encodes a {@link WorkoutDetail} into a binary Garmin .FIT file.

Mapping strategy (docs/07-workout-data-model.md §FIT File Export)

  • Repeat steps are flattened: inner steps first, followed by a {@code REPEAT_UNTIL_STEPS_CMPLT} sentinel step.
  • Zone-relative targets ({@code power_zone}, {@code hr_zone}, {@code pace_zone}) are passed through as zone references; the device resolves them against its zone tables.
  • Percentage-based targets ({@code power_pct}, {@code hr_pct}) are converted to absolute watts/bpm using the FTP/LTHR values supplied in the {@link ZoneContext}.
  • Absolute targets ({@code power_watts}, {@code hr_bpm}, {@code pace}, {@code speed}) are encoded directly.

FIT Profile unit conventions:

  • Power : watts
  • HR : bpm
  • Speed : mm/s (1 m/s = 1000 mm/s)
  • Time : milliseconds
  • Distance: centimeters

This class has no dependency on any other CoachFit module ({@code athlete}, {@code sync}, etc.) to keep it self-contained and testable in isolation.

methods.name: encode
methods.comment: Encodes the workout into binary FIT content using an in-memory {@link BufferEncoder}.

methods.name: flattenSteps
methods.comment: Flattens the step tree into a sequential list with repeat sentinels inserted. Message indices are assigned after the full list is built (0-based).

methods.name: expandRepeat
methods.comment:
Expands a {@code repeat} step: [inner steps…] + REPEAT sentinel.

The sentinel uses {@code REPEAT_UNTIL_STEPS_CMPLT} with:

  • {@code durationValue} = message_index of the first inner step
  • {@code targetValue} = repeat count

methods.name: buildLeafStep
methods.comment: Builds a single non-repeat {@link WorkoutStepMesg} from a step JSON node.

methods.name: applyTarget
methods.comment:
Maps an internal target descriptor to FIT step target fields.

Zone targets use SDK convenience setters (e.g., {@code setTargetPowerZone}) which internally set both {@code targetType} and {@code targetValue}. Custom ranges use {@code customTargetValueLow}/{@code High}.

methods.name: paceSecPerKmToMmPerSec
methods.comment: Pace in sec/km → speed in mm/s.

methods.name: kphToMmPerSec
methods.comment: km/h → mm/s.

name: com.coachfit.workout.domain.WorkoutStepsValidator
comment:
Validates the {@code steps} JSONB structure for a workout against the rules defined in docs/07-workout-data-model.md.

Rules

  • steps array must not be empty (length ≥ 1)
  • Valid step types: warmup, work, rest, cooldown, repeat, ramp, free
  • {@code repeat} steps must have a {@code count} (1–99) and a non-empty {@code steps[]} array
  • Max nesting depth is 1 — no repeat inside repeat
  • Non-repeat steps must have a {@code duration} object with {@code type} and (usually) {@code value}
  • Duration {@code value} must be > 0 (when required)
  • Target validation (when present):
    • power_zone: zone in 1–7
    • power_pct: 0.0 ≤ min ≤ max ≤ 3.0
    • power_watts: 0 ≤ min ≤ max ≤ 2000
    • hr_zone: zone in 1–5
    • hr_pct / hr_bpm: min ≤ max, HR 40–250 bpm
    • pace_zone: zone in 1–5
    • pace: min ≤ max, both > 0 sec/km
    • rpe: 1 ≤ min ≤ max ≤ 10

methods.name: validate
methods.comment: Validates the JSON string representing a workout's {@code steps} array.

name: com.coachfit.workout.domain.WorkoutValidationException
comment:
Thrown when a workout's {@code steps} JSON fails structural validation.

Caught by {@link com.coachfit.workout.adapter.in.WorkoutExceptionHandler} and mapped to HTTP 400 Bad Request.

name: com.coachfit.workout.domain.ZoneContext
comment:
Zone configuration context for FIT export target resolution.

Extracted from the athlete module's {@code SportZone} records and passed into {@link FitEncoder} so that domain code has no dependency on the athlete module.

methods.name: defaults
methods.comment: Returns a default context when the user has not configured any zones.
